summary: Deleted the global catch-all `src/functions.h` header and redistributed its forward declarations and function prototypes into more specific subsystem headers, adding local includes at the call sites that depended on them. The PR mattered because it reduced reliance on an undifferentiated prototype dump and moved declarations closer to their owning MSL, Runtime, Dolphin, item, fighter, stage, and HSD baselib headers.
what_changed: Removed `src/functions.h`, which had contained broad forward declarations plus prototypes grouped by pragma regions for item, Runtime, MSL string, OSAlloc, VI, HSD TObj, and HSD AObj symbols.
Moved MSL string prototypes such as `__StringRead`, `strchr`, `strncmp`, `strncpy`, `strcpy`, and `strlen` into `src/MSL/string.h`, with `ctypes.h` included for `unk_t`.
Moved Runtime conversion helper prototypes `__cvt_sll_flt` and `__cvt_dbl_usll` into `src/Runtime/Runtime.h`, and changed that header to include `ctypes.h` instead of `dolphin/types.h`.
Added `VIWaitForRetrace` to `src/dolphin/vi/vi.h` and an `OSAllocFromHeap(int, u32)` declaration to `src/dolphin/os/OSAlloc.h`.
Added item-related prototypes from `functions.h` to `src/melee/it/code_80266F3C.h`, `src/melee/it/code_8027CF30.h`, and `src/melee/it/item2.h`.
Moved `func_8026B560` from `item.h` to `item2.h`, keeping the existing comment that it is identical to `0x8026B54C` but likely uses a different `itAttributes` struct.

Added targeted includes in implementation files: `ftsamus3.c` and `ftcommon.c` include `melee/it/code_8027CF30.h`; `ftlib.c` includes `melee/it/item.h` and `melee/it/item2.h`; `ground.c` includes `melee/it/code_80266F3C.h` and `sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h`.
Added HSD baselib declarations `HSD_ForeachAnim(unk_t, ...)` to `aobj.h` and `HSD_ImageDescAlloc()` to `tobj.h`.
Used forward declarations for `HSD_GObj`, `HSD_JObj`, and `Vec` in item headers rather than pulling in broader Dolphin or HSD type headers where the excerpt shows only pointer usage.
smart_moves: Eliminated a monolithic global header by placing prototypes in subsystem-specific headers, improving ownership and making future decomp work easier to search by module.
Preserved build dependencies by adding narrow includes at the specific C files that needed the moved prototypes instead of keeping a project-wide convenience include.
Used forward declarations in `code_80266F3C.h` and `code_8027CF30.h` for pointer-only types, reducing include fan-out and avoiding unnecessary dependence on larger type headers.
Corrected header locality for `func_8026B560` by moving it from `item.h` to `item2.h`, consistent with the surrounding item2 function group in the deleted `functions.h` region.
Moved library/runtime symbols into their natural headers (`MSL/string.h`, `Runtime/Runtime.h`, `dolphin/vi/vi.h`, `OSAlloc.h`) instead of leaving them in a decomp-specific miscellaneous file.
decomp_lessons: Avoid accumulating unknown or cross-module prototypes in a catch-all `functions.h`; it hides ownership and makes include dependencies less explicit.
When deleting a convenience header, migrate each prototype to the closest owning subsystem header and then repair only the call sites that actually need it.
For pointer-only parameters, prefer forward declarations such as `typedef struct _HSD_GObj HSD_GObj;` over including broad type headers, unless full struct layout is required.
Keep comments attached to migrated prototypes so uncertain semantic notes, such as the `func_8026B560` relationship to `func_8026B54C`, are not lost during cleanup.
Header cleanup can be valuable even without matching new code because it clarifies boundaries between MSL, Runtime, Dolphin SDK, HSD baselib, and Melee gameplay modules.
A decomp project benefits when SDK/library functions live in SDK/library-style headers rather than game-specific aggregate headers.
naming_conventions: The deleted header used lowercase guard `_functions_h_`; subsystem headers retained their existing guards such as `MSL_STRING_H`, `RUNTIME_RUNTIME_H`, `MELEE_IT_CODE_80266F3C_H`, and `MELEE_IT_CODE_8027CF30_H`.
Unidentified game functions remained address-based names such as `func_8029A748`, `func_802B1DF8`, and `func_802F0AE0`; this PR did not rename them.
HSD struct forward declarations followed the existing underscore-tag pattern, e.g. `typedef struct _HSD_GObj HSD_GObj;` and `typedef struct _HSD_JObj HSD_JObj;`.
Standard/library prototypes used their canonical names, e.g. `strlen`, `strcpy`, `VIWaitForRetrace`, `OSAllocFromHeap`, `HSD_ImageDescAlloc`, and `HSD_ForeachAnim`.
Targeted includes use project angle-bracket paths such as `<melee/it/code_8027CF30.h>`, `<melee/it/item2.h>`, and `<sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic is documented in the PR text or diff excerpt.

The main matching-adjacent tactic was include/prototype hygiene: keep declarations available with minimal type dependencies, which can prevent compiler type or implicit-declaration changes from disturbing existing matches.

The diff shows includes inserted mid-file near functions that need them, a common decomp tactic when include ordering or local type availability affects matching or compilation; no explicit reviewer rationale was provided.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for continued removal of catch-all headers or further migration of address-named prototypes into subsystem headers.

Search whether `OSAllocFromHeap` kept duplicate/inconsistent prototypes after this PR, since `OSAlloc.h` already had `void *OSAllocFromHeap(OSHeapHandle, u32)` and this PR added `unk_t OSAllocFromHeap(int, u32)`.
Investigate whether `func_8026B560` later receives a semantic name or confirmed owning item attributes struct.
Search for remaining includes of `functions.h` after PR #590 to verify complete purge.
Look for later cleanup of mid-file includes in `ftcommon.c`, `ftlib.c`, `ftsamus3.c`, and `ground.c` once headers became more complete.
